DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Nov-13-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANTS JEFFREY GRAHAM, AT&T MOBILITY, LLC, NEW CINGULAR WIRELESS PCS, LLC'S DEMURRER to COMPLAINT:

When Shayna Lorenz bought a new cellphone, salesperson Taylor Pacera transferred her data from the old phone to the new one. In doing so, Pacera distributed sexual images of Lorenz's intimate body parts to third parties. Pacera was criminally charged and convicted. Lorenz sued Pacera, AT&T Mobility LLC New Cingular Wireless PCS, LLC and store manager Jeffrey Graham. With the exception of count 3, which is sustained with leave to amend, defendants' demurrers are overruled:
1: Invasion of Privacy. As defendants concede, "scope of employment is ordinarily" an issue "of fact," and the complaint pled that Pacera acted "in the scope of his work with AT&T." True, an employer is not liable for everything employees do during work hours. But downloading data to a customer's new phone is pled to be part of Pacera's job duties and even an "employee's unauthorized conduct may be within the scope of employment." (CACI 3722.)
2: Conversion. The complaint adequately pled interference with Lorenz's property; taking possession of the property suffices. (CACI 2100(2).)
3: Unfair Trade Practices. UCL claims alleging unauthorized release of personal information are rejected. (See, e.g., Fraley v. Facebook, Inc. (ND Cal. 2011) 830 F.Supp.2d 785, 811.) Given that, defendants' other points need not be addressed; however, Lorenz should consider them before re-filing this claim.
5: Aiding and Abetting Civil Theft. The complaint pled facts that met the elements of CACI 3610. A demurrer admits the truth of all facts alleged in a complaint no matter how unlikely. (Del E. Webb Corp. v. Structural Materials Co. (1981) 123 Cal.App.3d 593, 604.)
6&7: Fraud, Aiding and Abetting Fraud. Both were pled with sufficient particularity. (Cmplt. 18:24-24:11.)

order signed.

Judge: Richard Ulmer, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/RBU)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE

DEMURRER to COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Sep-10-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
No appearances. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT JEFFREY GRAHAM, AT&T MOBILITY, LLC, NEW CINGULAR WIRELESS PCS, LLC's DEMURRER to COMPLAINT is Off calendar.
